review_summary: This unit is well suited for core transformer-prep checks. The available text supports questions on self-attention Q/K/V projections, permutation equivariance, positional embeddings, masking, and multi-head computation.

question_bank.question: In self-attention, where do the queries, keys, and values come from?
choices: They are learned projections of the same set of input vectors.
They are manually labeled alignments supplied for every training example.
They are the previous, current, and next recurrent hidden states.
They are fixed sinusoidal constants that replace token embeddings.
question_bank.explanation: Self-attention uses one input set. Each input vector is linearly projected into query, key, and value roles so every element can attend to elements from the same set.

transcript_quotes: query, to a key, and to a value

question_bank.question: Why are positional embeddings needed when self-attention processes ordered sequences?
choices: Self-attention is permutation equivariant, so order information must be added separately.
Self-attention always reverses the sequence unless positions are provided.
Softmax cannot normalize attention scores without token indices.
Positional embeddings replace the need for keys and values.
question_bank.explanation: The self-attention computation treats inputs like a set: shuffling inputs shuffles outputs the same way. Positional information tells the model where each token or patch sits in the original order.

question_bank.question: How does masked self-attention prevent a position from attending to an illegal input?
choices: It sets selected alignment scores to negative infinity before softmax, making their weights zero.
It deletes those tokens from the batch before the model starts training.
It replaces all value vectors with the same learned constant.
It forces the attention head to use average pooling instead of softmax.
question_bank.explanation: A score of negative infinity becomes zero probability after softmax. That prevents the output from using value vectors at masked positions.
